text.title: stakeholder-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for stakeholder-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: PM centralizes all cross-functional communication through themselves (PM as bottleneck).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The register should route, not funnel — some engineering-to-sales communication should happen directly with the PM informed, not as a relay. Single-point-of-failure PMs create delays and knowledge silos.

failure-mode.description: PM escalates every decision upward, training executives to ignore communications and the team to bypass the PM.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Define explicitly what does NOT go upward as deliberately as what does; keep a written "I will decide" column on the decision log.

failure-mode.description: Lateral capture: a friendly head-of-sales becomes the loudest voice and silently steers the roadmap while appearing as one row among many in the register.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Track time spent per stakeholder monthly; if it does not match power x interest, recalibrate. The grid becomes inaccurate when attention diverges from the model.

failure-mode.description: Same communication format and content sent to all stakeholders regardless of role.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Executives need outcomes and risk; engineers need decisions and constraints; customers need benefits and how-to. One message serves none of them well.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring silent stakeholders — not proactively seeking input from those who don't speak up in meetings.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silent stakeholders who were not engaged become the loudest blockers at launch; they had concerns they never voiced because nobody asked.

failure-mode.description: Only sharing positive updates; hiding challenges until they are crises.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Executive trust is built by surfacing bad news early, not by delivering only good news — which trains executives to assume the PM is hiding problems.

failure-mode.description: No escalation path defined — no explicit process for resolving conflicts within a time limit.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without an escalation path, conflicts fester; an explicit "unresolved in 48h escalates to executive sponsor" creates the pressure needed to resolve things before they block delivery.

failure-mode.description: Tone-mismatched lateral messages: an LLM-drafted message to sales reads as eng-snobbery; the same draft to eng reads as sales-fluff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Generate per-audience variants from a structured source, never paraphrase a single draft into multiple audiences.

failure-mode.description: Calendar-scraping privacy: agents mining 1:1 transcripts to update attitude classifications have a high false-positive rate and cross legal lines in some jurisdictions (EU, CA).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Get explicit consent and surface the source quote with every classification change.

failure-mode.description: Persona-shifting exec proxies: when an exec's chief of staff is the actual decision proxy, an agent that addresses the exec by name in a brief routed through the CoS comes across as oblivious.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encode the proxy in the register and have the agent address by role, not name.

failure-mode.description: Multi-agent register drift: if both faion-pm-agent and faion-business-analyst write to the register, conflicting attitude classifications result.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Designate the PM agent as sole writer; BA proposes via a queue file (e.g. .aidocs/product/register-proposals.md) the PM agent reviews.

failure-mode.description: Long-context token waste: feeding the entire register into every prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Filter by comms_direction or decision_rights for the task at hand. A 200-row register dumped into every brief draft costs more than the briefs save.

failure-mode.description: Prompt-injection via meeting notes: hostile or careless attendees can paste content into shared notes that the agent ingests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat ingested transcripts as untrusted input; never let the agent take action (send mail, change register) without human confirmation.

failure-mode.description: Cross-team sentiment models are biased: tools that score Slack tone tend to over-flag direct cultures (German, Dutch, Israeli) as "Resistor".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Calibrate or disable; do not let cultural communication style be encoded as attitude.

failure-mode.description: Promotion-case fabrication: letting an agent draft your promotion narrative directly from the register; LLMs invent decisions you never made.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use the decision log + register to verify, write the narrative yourself.
